topic: Windsurf Browser Guide 2026: How Cascade Reads Your Browser Context

key_points: Windsurf Browser bridges IDE and browser to eliminate context switching
Everything visible in browser becomes instantly accessible to Cascade
Built as a Chromium fork with deep AI model integration
AI can track full timeline of developer actions across IDE and browser surfaces
Framed as 'digital pair programmer that can see your screen'
Context management is the core differentiator for Windsurf/Cascade
Four key context layers: Cascade engine, Rules files, Memories, @mentions
Cascade runs context assembly pipeline before LLM sees any prompt
Pipeline order: Load Rules → Load Memories → Read open files → Read recent actions
Tab completion uses separate context pipeline optimized for speed vs depth
Browser Previews allow viewing local app deployments inside IDE or system browser
Send Element button lets users select UI components and send to Cascade as @mentions
Console errors automatically captured and sendable to Cascade as context
Optimized for Chrome, Arc, and Chromium-based browsers
Listeners work in both embedded IDE view and external system browser
Cascade context engine tracks edits, terminal, clipboard, navigation patterns in real time
Web pages broken into chunks similar to how humans skim and read
Pages picked up from web search, inferred from conversation, or pasted URLs
Fast Context subagent retrieves codebase context 20x faster via SWE-grep models
Context pipeline assembles dynamically before each LLM call
Windsurf uniquely offers browser integration that Cursor lacks
Agent-integrated browser sends logs and elements directly to Cascade agent
Creates tight feedback loop for testing and previews without leaving IDE
Windsurf SWE-1.5 is 13x faster inference than Sonnet 4.5
Windsurf supports 40+ IDE plugins vs Cursor's single editor approach

stats.fact: Windsurf reached 1M+ active users by March 2026, with AI writing 70M+ lines of code every day
59% of Fortune 500 companies are building with Windsurf as of 2026
Windsurf ARR grew from $12M (Q4 2024) to $100M (Q3 2025) — one of the fastest enterprise expansions in AI tooling
94% of Windsurf's code output is AI-written; individual developers committed 44.6% AI-generated code on average
Fast Context subagent retrieves semantically relevant code up to 20x faster than traditional agentic search
Windsurf SWE-1.5 model achieves 13x faster inference than Sonnet 4.5 at near-frontier coding quality
Windsurf holds the #1 spot in LogRocket's AI Dev Tool Power Rankings as of February 2026, ahead of Cursor and GitHub Copilot
Cognition AI acquired Windsurf for approximately $250 million in December 2025

angles: The death of alt-tab: how Windsurf's browser integration keeps developers in 'flow state' without context switching

SWE-1 and browser context: how the custom AI model is trained specifically for developer browser patterns

MCP + Browser: extending Windsurf's browser context with custom tools (Playwright, Figma, etc.)
